text: BOOM: @IEA says UAE lifted its crude production to a **record high** of 4.1 million barrels a day in June (including condensates and natural gas liquids, total oil liquids output soared to an impressive 4.8m b/d).
quoted_text:
🚨🚨🚨 We have the first Persian Gulf country lifting its oil exports to (if not even above) pre-war levels: UAE.

Sure, the 'ketchup effect' may be inflating exports a bit (from inventories). But whatever that contribution is, clearly June oil exports have surged to >95% of pre-war levels (and some tanker trackers have UAE June oil exports even above pre-war levels).

With exports surging, the recovery in oil wellhead production won't be far behind, me thinks.

Importantly, UAE oil is flowing well

text: CHART OF THE DAY: Global oil stocks posted an increase in June (entirely due to rising volumes of oil on water), according to @IEA estimates.  It's the first monthly increase since the war, and comes after stocks fell ~360 million barres (or ~3.9m b/d) from March through May. https://t.co/uoH0D6kLfT
Iranian media reports that a Qatari delegation has arrived into the country for talks after days of strikes and counter strikes between the US and Iran.
Since hosting the COP30 climate change summit in Nov 2025, Brazilian oil production has surged ~500,000 b/d to an all-time high of 4.5 million b/d last month.
US President Donald Trump says it has agreed to continue talks with Iran. But he also reiterates the cease fire is over. https://t.co/eLDdVxDPPI

“…. The latest Middle East war has revealed many things about the global energy market, but among the most striking has been China's ability to upend traditional producer-consumer dynamics over oil prices…”

https://t.co/tm0QPQs3i3
